subject: English
chapter: Poem – The Tale of Custard the Dragon

activities_projects.title: Character Observation
activities_projects.objective: Identify the main characters and their traits.
steps: Read the poem carefully.
List all the characters mentioned in the poem.
Write 1–2 sentences describing each character.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize characters and basic personality traits.

activities_projects.title: Custard’s Courage
steps: Find the stanza where the pirate appears.
Describe what Custard does to defeat the pirate.
Write 3–4 sentences explaining why this act is brave.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the key event showing courage.

activities_projects.title: Finding Rhyming Words
steps: Read the poem aloud.
Find 10 pairs of rhyming words from the poem.
Write them in your notebook.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students identify rhymes and understand poetic sound.

activities_projects.title: Funny and Imaginative Lines
activities_projects.objective: Understand humor and imagination in the poem.
steps: Find 4–5 lines that sound funny or interesting.
Write them down.
Explain in one sentence why they are humorous.

activities_projects.title: Creative Drawing
steps: Draw Custard the dragon and Belinda’s house.
Include other characters like Mustard, Ink, Blink, and the pirate.
Write a short caption below the drawing.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students express understanding through visual creativity.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and relation to the poem.

activities_projects.title: Short Summary
activities_projects.objective: Summarize the poem in simple language.
steps: Read the poem once again.
Write the main events in 5–6 sentences.
Include the pirate scene and Custard’s bravery.

activities_projects.title: Role Play Activity
steps: Assign roles: Belinda, Custard, Mustard, Ink, Blink, and the pirate.
Act out the scene where the pirate enters the house.
Show each character’s reaction.

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Builder
activities_projects.objective: Learn new words from the poem.
steps: Find 5 new words from the poem (e.g., gaped, flustered).
Write their meanings.
Use each word in a simple sentence.

activities_projects.title: Character Comparison
steps: Write 3–4 sentences comparing Custard with Belinda, Mustard, Ink, and Blink.
Explain who is truly brave and why.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze characters in a simple way.

activities_projects.title: Reflection on Courage
activities_projects.objective: Relate the poem to personal experience.
steps: Think of a time when you were brave.
Write 4–5 sentences about your experience.
Explain how it is similar to Custard’s courage.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect literature with real-life experiences.
